mnemonics.title: Arrow Access
mnemonics.content: ptr->member = (*ptr).member - Think 'arrow points to member' like a road sign pointing to destination

acronyms.expansion: THis Instance Pointer (points to current object)
acronyms.usage: Used in member functions to refer to the object that invoked the function

acronyms.expansion: New Heap Allocation - Creates object in heap memory
Deallocate Heap - Frees heap memory and calls destructor

acronyms.usage: Initialize pointers when not pointing to valid object: ptr = nullptr;

visualTips.visualization: Draw object box with pointer arrow pointing to it. The arrow (->) is like a connector showing 'this pointer reaches that member'
Draw person holding mirror - 'this' is like person seeing themselves in mirror, the reflection is 'this' pointer pointing to self
Stack is like plates stacked (LIFO), Heap is like scattered boxes in storage - new places boxes in heap

analogies.analogy: Like house addresses - object is the house, pointer is the address card telling you where to find it
Like your own name - when someone says 'your name', you know they're referring to you; 'this' refers to the current object itself
new is like renting an apartment (allocation), delete is like moving out and returning keys (deallocation)
